text: as water-soluble minerals and vitamins get dissolved. There are many methods of cooking like boiling, steaming, pressure cooking, frying, roasting and baking. Boiling is the most common method of cooking, during which heat-labile and water-soluble vitamins like vitamin B-complex and C is lost. The practice of using excess water while cooking rice should be discouraged since it leads to loss of vitamins; just sufficient water to be fully absorbed should be used. Use of baking soda for hastening cooking of pulses should not be practiced, as it results in loss of vitamins. Frying involves cooking food in oil/ghee/ vanaspati at high temperatures. Shallow frying involves use of much smaller amounts of oils than deep frying. Repeated heating of oils particularly PUFA-rich oils results in
